-
MODE: READER

Here is the brief:

# Writ Parent/Child Relationships

Today, writs support arbitrary relationships between each other via 'links'. We would like to add a more structured, explicit relationship as well between "parent" writs and "child" writs. Parent/Child relationships would form a DAG, with each child having zero or one parents, and parents having zero or more children. Parent and child writs can be of any type -- they do not have to be of the same type.

There is a natural relationship between the status of parents and children. The following list is illustrative and and not necessarily exhaustive--during planning, the full state machine of parent/child hierarchies should be explored and designed, and may contradict the explicit examples below if a better design is selected.

- Parent writs are in a 'pending' or 'waiting' state if they have any children in non-terminal states. (Use suitable existing state or introduce new one)
- If any child writs fail, then all siblings should be cancelled/abandoned/something, and the parent should be failed as well
- when all children for a parent are completed, the parent should transition from pending/waiting into the ready state
- etc

Wherever possible, the writ state transitions should use CDC so that parent/sibling transitions happen in the same transaction.

---

Slug: writ-parentchild-relationships

1	/**
2	 * Clerk public types.
3	 *
4	 * All types exported from @shardworks/clerk-apparatus.
5	 */
6	
7	// ── Writ status ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8	
9	/**
10	 * A writ's position in its lifecycle.
11	 *
12	 * Transitions:
13	 *   new   → ready     (publish)   — draft held for review before entering the queue
14	 *   new   → cancelled (cancel)
15	 *   ready → active    (accept)
16	 *   active → completed (complete)
17	 *   active → failed    (fail)
18	 *   ready | active → cancelled (cancel)
19	 *
20	 * completed, failed, cancelled are terminal — no further transitions.
21	 */
22	export type WritStatus = 'new' | 'ready' | 'active' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
23	
24	// ── Documents ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25	
26	/**
27	 * A writ document as stored in The Stacks.
28	 */
29	export interface WritDoc {
30	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
31	  [key: string]: unknown;
32	  /** Unique writ id (`w-{base36_timestamp}{hex_random}`). Sortable by creation time. */
33	  id: string;
34	  /** Writ type — must be a type declared in guild config, or a built-in type. */
35	  type: string;
36	  /** Current lifecycle status. */
37	  status: WritStatus;
38	  /** Short human-readable title. */
39	  title: string;
40	  /** Detail text. */
41	  body: string;
42	  /** Target codex name. */
43	  codex?: string;
44	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ was created. */
45	  createdAt: string;
46	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
47	  updatedAt: string;
48	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ was accepted (transitioned to active). */
49	  acceptedAt?: string;
50	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ reached a terminal state. */
51	  resolvedAt?: string;
52	  /** Summary of how the writ resolved (set on any terminal transition). */
53	  resolution?: string;
54	}
55	
56	// ── Requests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57	
58	/**
59	 * Request to post a new commission (create a writ).
60	 */
61	export interface PostCommissionRequest {
62	  /**
63	   * Writ type. Defaults to the guild's configured defaultType, or "mandate"
64	   * if no default is configured. Must be a valid declared type.
65	   */
66	  type?: string;
67	  /** Short human-readable title describing the work. */
68	  title: string;
69	  /** Detail text. */
70	  body: string;
71	  /** Optional target codex name. */
72	  codex?: string;
73	  /**
74	   * When true, the writ is created in 'new' (draft) status instead of 'ready'.
75	   * Draft writs are invisible to the Spider and must be explicitly published
76	   * (new → ready) before they can be picked up for execution.
77	   * Defaults to false (writ enters the queue immediately).
78	   */
79	  draft?: boolean;
80	}
81	
82	// ── Filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
83	
84	/**
85	 * Filters for listing writs.
86	 */
87	export interface WritFilters {
88	  /** Filter by status. */
89	  status?: WritStatus;
90	  /** Filter by writ type. */
91	  type?: string;
92	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
93	  limit?: number;
94	  /** Number of results to skip. */
95	  offset?: number;
96	}
97	
98	// ── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────
99	
100	/**
101	 * A writ type entry declared in clerk config.
102	 */
103	export interface WritTypeEntry {
104	  /** The writ type name (e.g. "mandate", "task", "bug"). */
105	  name: string;
106	  /** Optional human-readable description of this writ type. */
107	  description?: string;
108	}
109	
110	/**
111	 * Clerk apparatus configuration — lives under the `clerk` key in guild.json.
112	 */
113	export interface ClerkConfig {
114	  /** Additional writ type declarations. The built-in type "mandate" is always valid. */
115	  writTypes?: WritTypeEntry[];
116	  /** Default writ type when commission-post is called without a type (default: "mandate"). */
117	  defaultType?: string;
118	}
119	
120	// Augment GuildConfig so `guild().guildConfig().clerk` is typed without
121	// requiring a manual type parameter at the call site.
122	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
123	  interface GuildConfig {
124	    clerk?: ClerkConfig;
125	  }
126	}
127	
128	// ── Link documents ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
129	
130	/**
131	 * A link document as stored in The Stacks (clerk/links book).
132	 */
133	export interface WritLinkDoc {
134	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
135	  [key: string]: unknown;
136	  /** Deterministic composite key: `{sourceId}:{targetId}:{type}`. */
137	  id: string;
138	  /** The writ that is the origin of this relationship. */
139	  sourceId: string;
140	  /** The writ that is the target of this relationship. */
141	  targetId: string;
142	  /** Relationship type — an open string (e.g. "fixes", "retries", "supersedes", "duplicates"). */
143	  type: string;
144	  /** ISO timestamp when the link was created. */
145	  createdAt: string;
146	}
147	
148	/**
149	 * Result of querying links for a writ — both directions in one response.
150	 */
151	export interface WritLinks {
152	  /** Links where this writ is the source (this writ → other writ). */
153	  outbound: WritLinkDoc[];
154	  /** Links where this writ is the target (other writ → this writ). */
155	  inbound: WritLinkDoc[];
156	}
157	
158	// ── API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
159	
160	/**
161	 * The Clerk's runtime API — retrieved via guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk').
162	 */
163	export interface ClerkApi {
164	  /**
165	   * Post a new commission, creating a writ in 'ready' status by default.
166	   * If `request.draft` is true, the writ is created in 'new' status instead
167	   * and will not be picked up by the Spider until explicitly published.
168	   * Validates the writ type against declared types in guild config.
169	   */
170	  post(request: PostCommissionRequest): Promise<WritDoc>;
171	
172	  /**
173	   * Show a writ by id. Throws if not found.
174	   */
175	  show(id: string): Promise<WritDoc>;
176	
177	  /**
178	   * List writs with optional filters, ordered by createdAt descending.
179	   */
180	  list(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<WritDoc[]>;
181	
182	  /**
183	   * Count writs matching optional filters.
184	   */
185	  count(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<number>;
186	
187	  /**
188	   * Transition a writ to a new status, optionally setting additional fields.
189	   * Validates that the transition is legal.
190	   */
191	  transition(id: string, to: WritStatus, fields?: Partial<WritDoc>): Promise<WritDoc>;
192	
193	  /**
194	   * Create a typed directional link from one writ to another.
195	   * Both writs must exist. Self-links are rejected. Idempotent — returns
196	   * the existing link if the (sourceId, targetId, type) triple already exists.
197	   */
198	  link(sourceId: string, targetId: string, type: string): Promise<WritLinkDoc>;
199	
200	  /**
201	   * Query all links for a writ — both outbound (this writ is the source)
202	   * and inbound (this writ is the target).
203	   */
204	  links(writId: string): Promise<WritLinks>;
205	
206	  /**
207	   * Remove a link. Idempotent — no error if the link does not exist.
208	   */
209	  unlink(sourceId: string, targetId: string, type: string): Promise<void>;
210	}

1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	import { guild } from '@shardworks/nexus-core';
3	import { tool } from '@shardworks/tools-apparatus';
4	import type { ClerkApi } from '../types.ts';
5	
6	export default tool({
7	  name: 'commission-post',
8	  description: 'Post a new commission, creating a writ in ready or new (draft) status',
9	  instructions:
10	    'Creates a new writ. By default the writ is placed in ready status and enters the queue ' +
11	    'immediately. Pass draft: true to create the writ in new (draft) status instead — draft ' +
12	    'writs are held out of the queue until explicitly published with writ-publish. ' +
13	    'The writ type must be a type declared in the guild config, or the built-in type "mandate". ' +
14	    'If type is omitted, the guild\'s configured default type is used (defaults to "mandate").',
15	  params: {
16	    title: z.string().describe('Short human-readable title describing the work'),
17	    body: z.string().describe('Detail text or description'),
18	    type: z.string().optional().describe('Writ type (default: guild defaultType or "mandate")'),
19	    codex: z.string().optional().describe('Target codex name'),
20	    draft: z
21	      .boolean()
22	      .optional()
23	      .describe(
24	        'When true, create the writ in new (draft) status instead of ready. ' +
25	        'Draft writs must be published before they enter the execution queue.',
26	      ),
27	  },
28	  permission: 'clerk:write',
29	  handler: async (params) => {
30	    const clerk = guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk');
31	    return clerk.post({
32	      title: params.title,
33	      body: params.body,
34	      type: params.type,
35	      codex: params.codex,
36	      draft: params.draft,
37	    });
38	  },
39	});

1	/**
2	 * @shardworks/clerk-apparatus — The Clerk.
3	 *
4	 * Writ lifecycle management: post commissions, accept work, complete or fail
5	 * writs, and cancel them at any pre-terminal stage. Writs flow through a fixed
6	 * status machine and are persisted in The Stacks.
7	 *
8	 * See: docs/architecture/apparatus/clerk.md
9	 */
10	
11	import { createClerk } from './clerk.ts';
12	
13	// ── Clerk API ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14	
15	export {
16	  type ClerkApi,
17	  type ClerkConfig,
18	  type WritTypeEntry,
19	  type WritDoc,
20	  type WritLinkDoc,
21	  type WritLinks,
22	  type WritStatus,
23	  type PostCommissionRequest,
24	  type WritFilters,
25	} from './types.ts';
26	
27	export { createClerk } from './clerk.ts';
28	export type { ClerkKit } from './clerk.ts';
29	
30	// ── Default export: the apparatus plugin ──────────────────────────────
31	
32	export default createClerk();
33

1	# The Clerk — API Contract
2	
3	Status: **Draft**
4	
5	Package: `@shardworks/clerk-apparatus` · Plugin id: `clerk`
6	
7	> **⚠️ MVP scope.** The first implementation covers flat mandate writs with patron-triggered dispatch. No writ hierarchy, no Clockworks integration. Future sections describe where this apparatus is headed once the Clockworks and rigging system exist.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	The Clerk is the guild's obligation authority. It receives commissions from the patron, issues writs that formally record what is owed, manages the lifecycle of those writs through to completion or failure, and maintains the Ledger — the guild's book of work.
14	
15	The Clerk owns the boundary between "what is asked for" and "how it gets done." A commission arrives; the Clerk creates a mandate writ. When work completes, the Clerk records the outcome. Connecting writs to sessions is the job of the rigging system — the Spider assembles rigs, and engines execute the work. The Clerk tracks the obligation, not the execution.
16	
17	The Clerk does **not** execute work. It does not launch sessions, manage rigs, or orchestrate engines. It tracks obligations: what has been commissioned, what state each obligation is in, and whether the guild has fulfilled its commitments. When the Clockworks and rigging system exist, the Clerk will integrate with them via lifecycle events and signals.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## Dependencies
22	
23	```
24	requires: ['stacks']
25	```
26	
27	- **The Stacks** (required) — persists writs in the `writs` book. All writ state lives here.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## Kit Interface
32	
33	The Clerk does not consume kit contributions. No `consumes` declaration.
34	
35	Kits that need to create or manage writs do so through the Clerk's tools or programmatic API, not through kit contribution fields. Writ creation is an operational act (with validation and lifecycle rules), not a declarative registration.
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Support Kit
40	
41	```typescript
42	supportKit: {
43	  books: {
44	    writs: {
45	      indexes: ['status', 'type', 'createdAt', ['status', 'type'], ['status', 'createdAt']],
46	    },
47	  },
48	  tools: [
49	    commissionPost,
50	    writShow,
51	    writList,
52	    writAccept,
53	    writComplete,
54	    writFail,
55	    writCancel,
56	    writPublish,
57	  ],
58	},
59	```
60	
61	### `commission-post` tool
62	
63	Post a new commission. Creates a writ in `ready` status by default, or in `new` (draft) status when `draft: true` is passed.
64	
65	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
66	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
67	| `title` | `string` | yes | Short description of the work |
68	| `body` | `string` | yes | Full spec — what to do, acceptance criteria, context |
69	| `codex` | `string` | no | Target codex name |
70	| `type` | `string` | no | Writ type (default: `"mandate"`) |
71	| `draft` | `boolean` | no | When `true`, create in `new` status — held out of the queue until published (default: `false`) |
72	
73	Returns the created `WritDoc`.
74	
75	Permission: `clerk:write`
76	
77	### `writ-show` tool
78	
79	Read a writ by id. Returns the full `WritDoc` including status history.
80	
81	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
82	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
83	| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
84	
85	Permission: `clerk:read`
86	
87	### `writ-list` tool
88	
89	List writs with optional filters. Returns writs ordered by `createdAt` descending.
90	
91	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
92	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
93	| `status` | `WritStatus` | no | Filter by status |
94	| `type` | `string` | no | Filter by writ type |
95	| `limit` | `number` | no | Max results (default: 20) |
96	
97	Permission: `clerk:read`
98	
99	### `writ-accept` tool
100	
101	Claim a writ. Transitions `ready → active`. Sets `acceptedAt`.
102	
103	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
104	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
105	| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
106	
107	Permission: `clerk:write`
108	
109	### `writ-complete` tool
110	
111	Mark a writ as successfully completed. Transitions `active → completed`.
112	
113	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
114	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
115	| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
116	| `resolution` | `string` | yes | What was done — summary of the work delivered |
117	
118	Permission: `clerk:write`
119	
120	### `writ-fail` tool
121	
122	Mark a writ as failed. Transitions `active → failed`.
123	
124	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
125	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
126	| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
127	| `resolution` | `string` | yes | Why the work failed |
128	
129	Permission: `clerk:write`
130	
131	### `writ-cancel` tool
132	
133	Cancel a writ. Transitions `new|ready|active → cancelled`.
134	
135	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
136	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
137	| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
138	| `resolution` | `string` | no | Why the writ was cancelled |
139	
140	Permission: `clerk:write`
141	
142	### `writ-publish` tool
143	
144	Publish a draft writ. Transitions `new → ready`, placing it in the execution queue.
145	
146	| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
147	|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
148	| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
149	
150	Returns the updated `WritDoc`.
151	
152	Permission: `clerk:write`
153	
154	---
155	
156	## `ClerkApi` Interface (`provides`)
157	
158	```typescript
159	interface ClerkApi {
160	  // ── Commission Intake ─────────────────────────────────────────
161	
162	  /**
163	   * Post a commission — create a writ in ready status (or new if draft: true).
164	   *
165	   * This is the primary entry point for patron-originated work.
166	   * Creates a WritDoc and persists it to the writs book.
167	   * Draft writs (new status) are invisible to the Spider until published.
168	   */
169	  post(request: PostCommissionRequest): Promise<WritDoc>
170	
171	  // ── Writ Queries ──────────────────────────────────────────────
172	
173	  /** Read a single writ by id. Throws if not found. */
174	  show(id: string): Promise<WritDoc>
175	
176	  /** List writs with optional filters. */
177	  list(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<WritDoc[]>
178	
179	  /** Count writs matching filters. */
180	  count(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<number>
181	
182	  // ── Writ Lifecycle ────────────────────────────────────────────
183	
184	  /**
185	   * Transition a writ to a new status.
186	   *
187	   * Enforces the status machine — invalid transitions throw.
188	   * Updates the writ document and sets timestamp fields.
189	   *
190	   * Valid transitions:
191	   *   new   → ready      (publish — enter the queue)
192	   *   new   → cancelled
193	   *   ready → active
194	   *   active → completed
195	   *   active → failed
196	   *   ready|active → cancelled
197	   *
198	   * The `fields` parameter allows setting additional fields
199	   * atomically with the transition (e.g. `resolution`).
200	   */
201	  transition(id: string, to: WritStatus, fields?: Partial<WritDoc>): Promise<WritDoc>
202	}
203	```
204	
205	### Supporting Types
206	
207	```typescript
208	interface WritDoc {
209	  /** Unique writ id (prefixed, sortable: `w-{base36_timestamp}{hex_random}`). */
210	  id: string
211	  /** Writ type — guild vocabulary. e.g. "mandate", "task", "bug". */
212	  type: string
213	  /** Current status. */
214	  status: WritStatus
215	  /** Short description. */
216	  title: string
217	  /** Full spec — what to do, acceptance criteria, context. */
218	  body: string
219	  /** Target codex name, if applicable. */
220	  codex?: string
221	
222	  // ── Timestamps ──────────────────────────────────────────────
223	
224	  /** When the writ was created. */
225	  createdAt: string
226	  /** When the writ was last modified. */
227	  updatedAt: string
228	  /** When status moved to active (accepted). */
229	  acceptedAt?: string
230	  /** When terminal status was reached. */
231	  resolvedAt?: string
232	
233	  // ── Resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────
234	
235	  /** Summary of how the writ resolved. Set on any terminal transition.
236	   *  What was done (completed), why it failed (failed), or why it
237	   *  was cancelled (cancelled). The `status` field distinguishes which. */
238	  resolution?: string
239	}
240	
241	type WritStatus =
242	  | "new"         // Draft — held out of the queue, not yet published
243	  | "ready"       // In the queue, awaiting acceptance or dispatch
244	  | "active"      // Claimed by an anima, work in progress
245	  | "completed"   // Work done successfully
246	  | "failed"      // Work failed
247	  | "cancelled"   // Cancelled by patron or system
248	
249	interface PostCommissionRequest {
250	  title: string
251	  body: string
252	  codex?: string
253	  type?: string       // default: "mandate"
254	  draft?: boolean     // When true, create in 'new' status (default: false → 'ready')
255	}
256	
257	interface WritFilters {
258	  status?: WritStatus
259	  type?: string
260	  limit?: number
261	  offset?: number
262	}
263	```
264	
265	---
266	
267	## Configuration
268	
269	All Clerk configuration lives under the `clerk` key in `guild.json`. The Clerk uses [module augmentation](../plugins.md#typed-config-via-module-augmentation-recommended) to extend `GuildConfig`, so config is accessed via `guild().guildConfig().clerk` with full type safety — no manual cast needed.
270	
271	```json
272	{
273	  "clerk": {
274	    "writTypes": [
275	      { "name": "mandate" },
276	      { "name": "task", "description": "A concrete unit of implementation work" },
277	      { "name": "bug", "description": "A defect to investigate and fix" }
278	    ],
279	    "defaultType": "mandate"
280	  }
281	}
282	```
283	
284	```typescript
285	interface ClerkConfig {
286	  writTypes?: WritTypeEntry[]
287	  defaultType?: string
288	}
289	
290	interface WritTypeEntry {
291	  name: string
292	  description?: string
293	}
294	
295	// Module augmentation — typed access via guild().guildConfig().clerk
296	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
297	  interface GuildConfig {
298	    clerk?: ClerkConfig
299	  }
300	}
301	```
302	
303	| Field | Type | Default | Description |
304	|-------|------|---------|-------------|
305	| `writTypes` | `WritTypeEntry[]` | `[]` | Additional writ type declarations. Each entry has a `name` and optional `description`. The built-in type `"mandate"` is always valid regardless of this list. |
306	| `defaultType` | `string` | `"mandate"` | Default type when `commission-post` is called without a type. |
307	
308	Both fields are optional. A guild with no `clerk` config (or an empty one) gets only the built-in `mandate` type with `defaultType: "mandate"` — enough to post commissions with no configuration.
309	
310	Writ types are the guild's vocabulary — not a framework-imposed hierarchy. A guild that does only implementation work might use only `mandate`. A guild with planning animas might add `task`, `step`, `bug`, `spike`. The Clerk validates that posted writs use a declared type but assigns no behavioral semantics to the type name — that meaning lives in role instructions and (when available) standing orders and engine designs.
311	
312	---
313	
314	## Status Machine
315	
316	The writ status machine governs all transitions. The Clerk enforces this — invalid transitions throw.
317	
318	```
319	            ┌──────────────┐
320	            │     new      │──────────────────┐
321	            └──────┬───────┘                  │
322	                   │                          │
323	               publish                     cancel
324	                   │                          │
325	                   ▼                          │
326	            ┌──────────────┐                  │
327	            │    ready     │──────────┐       │
328	            └──────┬───────┘          │       │
329	                   │                  │       │
330	              accept               cancel     │
331	                   │                  │       │
332	                   ▼                  │       │
333	            ┌──────────────┐          │       │
334	            │    active    │──────┐   │       │
335	            └──┬───────┬───┘      │   │       │
336	               │       │          │   │       │
337	          complete    fail     cancel  │       │
338	               │       │          │   │       │
339	               ▼       ▼          │   │       │
340	        ┌───────────┐ ┌────────┐  │   │       │
341	        │ completed │ │ failed │  │   │       │
342	        └───────────┘ └────────┘  │   │       │
343	                                  │   │       │
344	              ┌───────────┐       │   │       │
345	              │ cancelled │◀──────┘   │       │
346	              │           │◀──────────┘       │
347	              │           │◀──────────────────┘
348	              └───────────┘
349	```
350	
351	Terminal statuses: `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`. No transitions out of terminal states.
352	
353	The `new` status is a pre-queue holding state. A writ in `new` status:
354	- Is **not** visible to the Spider's spawn phase (which queries exclusively for `ready` writs)
355	- Can be reviewed, linked to other writs, and edited before entering the queue
356	- Must be explicitly published (`new → ready`) via the `writ-publish` tool before it will be picked up
357	- Can be cancelled directly from `new` without ever entering the queue
358	
359	### [Future] The `pending` status
360	
361	When writ hierarchy is implemented, a parent writ transitions to `pending` when it has active children and is not directly actionable itself. `pending` is not a terminal state — when all children complete, the parent can transition to `completed`. If any child fails, the parent can transition to `failed`.
362	
363	```
364	ready → pending    (when children are created via decompose())
365	pending → completed  (when all children complete — may be automatic)
366	pending → failed     (when a child fails — patron decides)
367	pending → cancelled
368	```
369	
370	---
371	
372	## Commission Intake Pipeline
373	
374	Commission intake is a single synchronous step:
375	
376	```
377	├─ 1. Patron calls commission-post (or ClerkApi.post())
378	├─ 2. Clerk validates input, generates ULID, creates WritDoc
379	├─ 3a. draft: false (default) → Clerk writes WritDoc with status: ready
380	│       └─ Spider will pick up on next crawl tick
381	└─ 3b. draft: true → Clerk writes WritDoc with status: new
382	        └─ Held out of queue; patron calls writ-publish to enter queue
383	```
384	
385	One commission = one mandate writ. No planning, no decomposition. Execution is handled by the Spider, which spawns a rig for each **ready** writ and drives it through the engine pipeline. Writs in `new` status are invisible to the Spider until published.
386	
387	---
388	
389	## Future: Clockworks Integration
390	
391	When the Clockworks apparatus exists, the Clerk gains event emission and reactive dispatch.
392	
393	### Dependency Change
394	
395	```
396	requires:   ['stacks']
397	recommends: ['clockworks']
398	```
399	
400	The Clockworks becomes a recommended (not required) dependency. The Clerk checks for the Clockworks at emit time — not at startup — so it functions standalone. When the Clockworks is absent, event emission is silently skipped.
401	
402	### Lifecycle Events
403	
404	The Clerk emits events into the Clockworks event stream at each status transition. Event names use the writ's `type` as the namespace, matching the framework event catalog.
405	
406	| Transition | Event | Payload |
407	|-----------|-------|---------|
408	| Commission posted | `commission.posted` | `{ writId, title, type, codex }` |
409	| Writ signaled ready | `{type}.ready` | `{ writId, title, type, codex }` |
410	| `ready → active` | `{type}.active` | `{ writId }` |
411	| `active → completed` | `{type}.completed` | `{ writId, resolution }` |
412	| `active → failed` | `{type}.failed` | `{ writId, resolution }` |
413	| `* → cancelled` | `{type}.cancelled` | `{ writId, resolution }` |
414	
415	These events are what standing orders bind to. The canonical dispatch pattern:
416	
417	```json
418	{
419	  "clockworks": {
420	    "standingOrders": [
421	      { "on": "mandate.ready", "summon": "artificer", "prompt": "Read your writ with writ-show and fulfill the commission. Writ id: {{writ.id}}" }
422	    ]
423	  }
424	}
425	```
426	
427	### `signal()` Method
428	
429	A new method on `ClerkApi`:
430	
431	```typescript
432	/**
433	 * Signal that a writ is ready for dispatch.
434	 *
435	 * Emits `{type}.ready` into the Clockworks event stream.
436	 * In the full design, called after intake processing (Sage
437	 * decomposition, validation) completes. This is the signal
438	 * the Spider (or summon relay) listens for to begin execution.
439	 */
440	signal(id: string): Promise<void>
441	```
442	
443	### Execution Integration
444	
445	The Clerk integrates with the execution layer at two points:
446	
447	**Outbound: Ready Signal.** When a writ is signaled ready, the Clockworks event stream carries it to standing orders. The Spider picks it up and spawns a rig to begin work. The Clerk does not know or care how the writ is executed — it signals readiness; the guild's configuration determines the response.
448	
449	**Inbound: Completion Signal.** When work completes, the completing apparatus calls `clerk.transition(id, 'completed', { outcome })`. The Spider calls this when it strikes a completed rig. For direct-summon execution (standing orders), the anima calls `writ-complete` (which wraps `clerk.transition()`). Both paths converge on the same Clerk API.
450	
451	### Intake with Planning
452	
453	When Sage animas and the Clockworks are available, the intake pipeline gains a planning step:
454	
455	```
456	├─ 1. Patron calls commission-post
457	├─ 2. Clerk creates mandate writ (status: ready)
458	├─ 3. Clerk emits commission.posted
459	├─ 4. Standing order on commission.posted summons a Sage
460	├─ 5. Sage reads the mandate, decomposes into child writs via decompose()
461	├─ 6. Clerk creates child writs (status: ready), sets parent to pending
462	├─ 7. Clerk emits {childType}.ready for each child
463	├─ 8. Standing orders on {childType}.ready dispatch workers
464	├─ 9. As children complete, Clerk rolls up status to parent
465	└─ 10. When all children complete, parent mandate → completed
466	```
467	
468	The patron's experience doesn't change — they still call `commission-post`. The planning step is internal to the guild.
469	
470	---
471	
472	## Future: Writ Hierarchy
473	
474	Writs form a tree. A mandate writ may be decomposed into child writs (tasks, steps, etc.) by a planning anima. The hierarchy enables:
475	
476	- **Decomposition** — a broad commission broken into concrete tasks
477	- **Completion rollup** — parent completes when all children complete
478	- **Failure propagation** — parent awareness of child failures
479	- **Scope tracking** — the patron sees one mandate; the guild sees the tree
480	
481	### Hierarchy Rules
482	
483	- A writ may have zero or one parent.
484	- A writ may have zero or many children.
485	- Depth is not limited (but deep hierarchies are a design smell).
486	- Children inherit the parent's `codex` unless explicitly overridden.
487	- The parent's `childCount` is denormalized and maintained by the Clerk.
488	
489	### Completion Rollup
490	
491	When a child writ reaches a terminal status, the Clerk checks siblings:
492	- All children `completed` → parent auto-transitions to `completed`
493	- Any child `failed` → the Clerk emits `{parentType}.child-failed` but does NOT auto-fail the parent. The patron (or a standing order) decides whether to fail, retry, or cancel.
494	- Child `cancelled` → no automatic parent transition.
495	
496	### `decompose()` Method
497	
498	```typescript
499	/**
500	 * Create child writs under a parent.
501	 *
502	 * Used by planning animas (Sages) to decompose a mandate into
503	 * concrete tasks. Children inherit the parent's codex unless
504	 * overridden. The parent transitions to `pending` when it has
505	 * active children and is not directly actionable.
506	 */
507	decompose(parentId: string, children: CreateWritRequest[]): Promise<WritDoc[]>
508	```
509	
510	---
511	
512	## Open Questions
513	
514	- **Should `commission-post` be a permissionless tool?** It represents patron authority — commissions come from outside the guild. But Coco (running inside a session) needs to call it. Current thinking: gate it with `clerk:write` and grant that to the steward role.
515	
516	- **Writ type validation — strict or advisory?** The Clerk validates against `clerk.writTypes` in config. But this means adding a new type requires a config change. Alternative: accept any string, use the config list only for documentation/tooling hints. Current thinking: strict validation — the guild should know its own vocabulary.
517	
518	---
519	
520	## Implementation Notes
521	
522	- Standalone apparatus package at `packages/plugins/clerk/`. Requires only the Stacks.
523	- `WritDoc.type` uses a guild-defined vocabulary, not a framework enum. The Clerk validates against `clerk.writTypes` in the apparatus config section but the framework imposes no meaning on the type name.
524	- Writ ids use the format `w-{base36_timestamp}{hex_random}` — sortable by creation time, unique without coordination. Not a formal ULID, but provides the same useful properties (temporal ordering, no coordination).
525	- The `transition()` method is the single choke point for all status changes. All tools and future integrations go through it. This is where validation, timestamp setting, and (future) event emission and hierarchy rollup happen.
526	- When the Clockworks is eventually added as a recommended dependency, resolve it at emit time via `guild().apparatus()`, not at startup — so the Clerk functions with or without it.
527

# The Clerk — API Contract

Status: **Draft**

Package: `@shardworks/clerk-apparatus` · Plugin id: `clerk`

> **⚠️ MVP scope.** The first implementation covers flat mandate writs with patron-triggered dispatch. No writ hierarchy, no Clockworks integration. Future sections describe where this apparatus is headed once the Clockworks and rigging system exist.

---

## Purpose

The Clerk is the guild's obligation authority. It receives commissions from the patron, issues writs that formally record what is owed, manages the lifecycle of those writs through to completion or failure, and maintains the Ledger — the guild's book of work.

The Clerk owns the boundary between "what is asked for" and "how it gets done." A commission arrives; the Clerk creates a mandate writ. When work completes, the Clerk records the outcome. Connecting writs to sessions is the job of the rigging system — the Spider assembles rigs, and engines execute the work. The Clerk tracks the obligation, not the execution.

The Clerk does **not** execute work. It does not launch sessions, manage rigs, or orchestrate engines. It tracks obligations: what has been commissioned, what state each obligation is in, and whether the guild has fulfilled its commitments. When the Clockworks and rigging system exist, the Clerk will integrate with them via lifecycle events and signals.

---

## Dependencies

```
requires: ['stacks']
```

- **The Stacks** (required) — persists writs in the `writs` book. All writ state lives here.

---

## Kit Interface

The Clerk does not consume kit contributions. No `consumes` declaration.

Kits that need to create or manage writs do so through the Clerk's tools or programmatic API, not through kit contribution fields. Writ creation is an operational act (with validation and lifecycle rules), not a declarative registration.

---

## Support Kit

```typescript
supportKit: {
  books: {
    writs: {
      indexes: ['status', 'type', 'createdAt', ['status', 'type'], ['status', 'createdAt']],
    },
  },
  tools: [
    commissionPost,
    writShow,
    writList,
    writAccept,
    writComplete,
    writFail,
    writCancel,
    writPublish,
  ],
},
```

### `commission-post` tool

Post a new commission. Creates a writ in `ready` status by default, or in `new` (draft) status when `draft: true` is passed.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | `string` | yes | Short description of the work |
| `body` | `string` | yes | Full spec — what to do, acceptance criteria, context |
| `codex` | `string` | no | Target codex name |
| `type` | `string` | no | Writ type (default: `"mandate"`) |
| `draft` | `boolean` | no | When `true`, create in `new` status — held out of the queue until published (default: `false`) |

Returns the created `WritDoc`.

Permission: `clerk:write`

### `writ-show` tool

Read a writ by id. Returns the full `WritDoc` including status history.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |

Permission: `clerk:read`

### `writ-list` tool

List writs with optional filters. Returns writs ordered by `createdAt` descending.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `status` | `WritStatus` | no | Filter by status |
| `type` | `string` | no | Filter by writ type |
| `limit` | `number` | no | Max results (default: 20) |

Permission: `clerk:read`

### `writ-accept` tool

Claim a writ. Transitions `ready → active`. Sets `acceptedAt`.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |

Permission: `clerk:write`

### `writ-complete` tool

Mark a writ as successfully completed. Transitions `active → completed`.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
| `resolution` | `string` | yes | What was done — summary of the work delivered |

Permission: `clerk:write`

### `writ-fail` tool

Mark a writ as failed. Transitions `active → failed`.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
| `resolution` | `string` | yes | Why the work failed |

Permission: `clerk:write`

### `writ-cancel` tool

Cancel a writ. Transitions `new|ready|active → cancelled`.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |
| `resolution` | `string` | no | Why the writ was cancelled |

Permission: `clerk:write`

### `writ-publish` tool

Publish a draft writ. Transitions `new → ready`, placing it in the execution queue.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | `string` | yes | Writ id |

Returns the updated `WritDoc`.

Permission: `clerk:write`

---

## `ClerkApi` Interface (`provides`)

```typescript
interface ClerkApi {
  // ── Commission Intake ─────────────────────────────────────────

  /**
   * Post a commission — create a writ in ready status (or new if draft: true).
   *
   * This is the primary entry point for patron-originated work.
   * Creates a WritDoc and persists it to the writs book.
   * Draft writs (new status) are invisible to the Spider until published.
   */
  post(request: PostCommissionRequest): Promise<WritDoc>

  // ── Writ Queries ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  /** Read a single writ by id. Throws if not found. */
  show(id: string): Promise<WritDoc>

  /** List writs with optional filters. */
  list(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<WritDoc[]>

  /** Count writs matching filters. */
  count(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<number>

  // ── Writ Lifecycle ────────────────────────────────────────────

  /**
   * Transition a writ to a new status.
   *
   * Enforces the status machine — invalid transitions throw.
   * Updates the writ document and sets timestamp fields.
   *
   * Valid transitions:
   *   new   → ready      (publish — enter the queue)
   *   new   → cancelled
   *   ready → active
   *   active → completed
   *   active → failed
   *   ready|active → cancelled
   *
   * The `fields` parameter allows setting additional fields
   * atomically with the transition (e.g. `resolution`).
   */
  transition(id: string, to: WritStatus, fields?: Partial<WritDoc>): Promise<WritDoc>
}
```

### Supporting Types

```typescript
interface WritDoc {
  /** Unique writ id (prefixed, sortable: `w-{base36_timestamp}{hex_random}`). */
  id: string
  /** Writ type — guild vocabulary. e.g. "mandate", "task", "bug". */
  type: string
  /** Current status. */
  status: WritStatus
  /** Short description. */
  title: string
  /** Full spec — what to do, acceptance criteria, context. */
  body: string
  /** Target codex name, if applicable. */
  codex?: string

  // ── Timestamps ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  /** When the writ was created. */
  createdAt: string
  /** When the writ was last modified. */
  updatedAt: string
  /** When status moved to active (accepted). */
  acceptedAt?: string
  /** When terminal status was reached. */
  resolvedAt?: string

  // ── Resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────

  /** Summary of how the writ resolved. Set on any terminal transition.
   *  What was done (completed), why it failed (failed), or why it
   *  was cancelled (cancelled). The `status` field distinguishes which. */
  resolution?: string
}

type WritStatus =
  | "new"         // Draft — held out of the queue, not yet published
  | "ready"       // In the queue, awaiting acceptance or dispatch
  | "active"      // Claimed by an anima, work in progress
  | "completed"   // Work done successfully
  | "failed"      // Work failed
  | "cancelled"   // Cancelled by patron or system

interface PostCommissionRequest {
  title: string
  body: string
  codex?: string
  type?: string       // default: "mandate"
  draft?: boolean     // When true, create in 'new' status (default: false → 'ready')
}

interface WritFilters {
  status?: WritStatus
  type?: string
  limit?: number
  offset?: number
}
```

---

## Configuration

All Clerk configuration lives under the `clerk` key in `guild.json`. The Clerk uses [module augmentation](../plugins.md#typed-config-via-module-augmentation-recommended) to extend `GuildConfig`, so config is accessed via `guild().guildConfig().clerk` with full type safety — no manual cast needed.

```json
{
  "clerk": {
    "writTypes": [
      { "name": "mandate" },
      { "name": "task", "description": "A concrete unit of implementation work" },
      { "name": "bug", "description": "A defect to investigate and fix" }
    ],
    "defaultType": "mandate"
  }
}
```

```typescript
interface ClerkConfig {
  writTypes?: WritTypeEntry[]
  defaultType?: string
}

interface WritTypeEntry {
  name: string
  description?: string
}

// Module augmentation — typed access via guild().guildConfig().clerk
declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
  interface GuildConfig {
    clerk?: ClerkConfig
  }
}
```

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `writTypes` | `WritTypeEntry[]` | `[]` | Additional writ type declarations. Each entry has a `name` and optional `description`. The built-in type `"mandate"` is always valid regardless of this list. |
| `defaultType` | `string` | `"mandate"` | Default type when `commission-post` is called without a type. |

Both fields are optional. A guild with no `clerk` config (or an empty one) gets only the built-in `mandate` type with `defaultType: "mandate"` — enough to post commissions with no configuration.

Writ types are the guild's vocabulary — not a framework-imposed hierarchy. A guild that does only implementation work might use only `mandate`. A guild with planning animas might add `task`, `step`, `bug`, `spike`. The Clerk validates that posted writs use a declared type but assigns no behavioral semantics to the type name — that meaning lives in role instructions and (when available) standing orders and engine designs.

---

## Status Machine

The writ status machine governs all transitions. The Clerk enforces this — invalid transitions throw.

```
            ┌──────────────┐
            │     new      │──────────────────┐
            └──────┬───────┘                  │
                   │                          │
               publish                     cancel
                   │                          │
                   ▼                          │
            ┌──────────────┐                  │
            │    ready     │──────────┐       │
            └──────┬───────┘          │       │
                   │                  │       │
              accept               cancel     │
                   │                  │       │
                   ▼                  │       │
            ┌──────────────┐          │       │
            │    active    │──────┐   │       │
            └──┬───────┬───┘      │   │       │
               │       │          │   │       │
          complete    fail     cancel  │       │
               │       │          │   │       │
               ▼       ▼          │   │       │
        ┌───────────┐ ┌────────┐  │   │       │
        │ completed │ │ failed │  │   │       │
        └───────────┘ └────────┘  │   │       │
                                  │   │       │
              ┌───────────┐       │   │       │
              │ cancelled │◀──────┘   │       │
              │           │◀──────────┘       │
              │           │◀──────────────────┘
              └───────────┘
```

Terminal statuses: `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`. No transitions out of terminal states.

The `new` status is a pre-queue holding state. A writ in `new` status:
- Is **not** visible to the Spider's spawn phase (which queries exclusively for `ready` writs)
- Can be reviewed, linked to other writs, and edited before entering the queue
- Must be explicitly published (`new → ready`) via the `writ-publish` tool before it will be picked up
- Can be cancelled directly from `new` without ever entering the queue

### [Future] The `pending` status

When writ hierarchy is implemented, a parent writ transitions to `pending` when it has active children and is not directly actionable itself. `pending` is not a terminal state — when all children complete, the parent can transition to `completed`. If any child fails, the parent can transition to `failed`.

```
ready → pending    (when children are created via decompose())
pending → completed  (when all children complete — may be automatic)
pending → failed     (when a child fails — patron decides)
pending → cancelled
```

---

## Commission Intake Pipeline

Commission intake is a single synchronous step:

```
├─ 1. Patron calls commission-post (or ClerkApi.post())
├─ 2. Clerk validates input, generates ULID, creates WritDoc
├─ 3a. draft: false (default) → Clerk writes WritDoc with status: ready
│       └─ Spider will pick up on next crawl tick
└─ 3b. draft: true → Clerk writes WritDoc with status: new
        └─ Held out of queue; patron calls writ-publish to enter queue
```

One commission = one mandate writ. No planning, no decomposition. Execution is handled by the Spider, which spawns a rig for each **ready** writ and drives it through the engine pipeline. Writs in `new` status are invisible to the Spider until published.

---

## Future: Clockworks Integration

When the Clockworks apparatus exists, the Clerk gains event emission and reactive dispatch.

### Dependency Change

```
requires:   ['stacks']
recommends: ['clockworks']
```

The Clockworks becomes a recommended (not required) dependency. The Clerk checks for the Clockworks at emit time — not at startup — so it functions standalone. When the Clockworks is absent, event emission is silently skipped.

### Lifecycle Events

The Clerk emits events into the Clockworks event stream at each status transition. Event names use the writ's `type` as the namespace, matching the framework event catalog.

| Transition | Event | Payload |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| Commission posted | `commission.posted` | `{ writId, title, type, codex }` |
| Writ signaled ready | `{type}.ready` | `{ writId, title, type, codex }` |
| `ready → active` | `{type}.active` | `{ writId }` |
| `active → completed` | `{type}.completed` | `{ writId, resolution }` |
| `active → failed` | `{type}.failed` | `{ writId, resolution }` |
| `* → cancelled` | `{type}.cancelled` | `{ writId, resolution }` |

These events are what standing orders bind to. The canonical dispatch pattern:

```json
{
  "clockworks": {
    "standingOrders": [
      { "on": "mandate.ready", "summon": "artificer", "prompt": "Read your writ with writ-show and fulfill the commission. Writ id: {{writ.id}}" }
    ]
  }
}
```

### `signal()` Method

A new method on `ClerkApi`:

```typescript
/**
 * Signal that a writ is ready for dispatch.
 *
 * Emits `{type}.ready` into the Clockworks event stream.
 * In the full design, called after intake processing (Sage
 * decomposition, validation) completes. This is the signal
 * the Spider (or summon relay) listens for to begin execution.
 */
signal(id: string): Promise<void>
```

### Execution Integration

The Clerk integrates with the execution layer at two points:

**Outbound: Ready Signal.** When a writ is signaled ready, the Clockworks event stream carries it to standing orders. The Spider picks it up and spawns a rig to begin work. The Clerk does not know or care how the writ is executed — it signals readiness; the guild's configuration determines the response.

**Inbound: Completion Signal.** When work completes, the completing apparatus calls `clerk.transition(id, 'completed', { outcome })`. The Spider calls this when it strikes a completed rig. For direct-summon execution (standing orders), the anima calls `writ-complete` (which wraps `clerk.transition()`). Both paths converge on the same Clerk API.

### Intake with Planning

When Sage animas and the Clockworks are available, the intake pipeline gains a planning step:

```
├─ 1. Patron calls commission-post
├─ 2. Clerk creates mandate writ (status: ready)
├─ 3. Clerk emits commission.posted
├─ 4. Standing order on commission.posted summons a Sage
├─ 5. Sage reads the mandate, decomposes into child writs via decompose()
├─ 6. Clerk creates child writs (status: ready), sets parent to pending
├─ 7. Clerk emits {childType}.ready for each child
├─ 8. Standing orders on {childType}.ready dispatch workers
├─ 9. As children complete, Clerk rolls up status to parent
└─ 10. When all children complete, parent mandate → completed
```

The patron's experience doesn't change — they still call `commission-post`. The planning step is internal to the guild.

---

## Future: Writ Hierarchy

Writs form a tree. A mandate writ may be decomposed into child writs (tasks, steps, etc.) by a planning anima. The hierarchy enables:

- **Decomposition** — a broad commission broken into concrete tasks
- **Completion rollup** — parent completes when all children complete
- **Failure propagation** — parent awareness of child failures
- **Scope tracking** — the patron sees one mandate; the guild sees the tree

### Hierarchy Rules

- A writ may have zero or one parent.
- A writ may have zero or many children.
- Depth is not limited (but deep hierarchies are a design smell).
- Children inherit the parent's `codex` unless explicitly overridden.
- The parent's `childCount` is denormalized and maintained by the Clerk.

### Completion Rollup

When a child writ reaches a terminal status, the Clerk checks siblings:
- All children `completed` → parent auto-transitions to `completed`
- Any child `failed` → the Clerk emits `{parentType}.child-failed` but does NOT auto-fail the parent. The patron (or a standing order) decides whether to fail, retry, or cancel.
- Child `cancelled` → no automatic parent transition.

### `decompose()` Method

```typescript
/**
 * Create child writs under a parent.
 *
 * Used by planning animas (Sages) to decompose a mandate into
 * concrete tasks. Children inherit the parent's codex unless
 * overridden. The parent transitions to `pending` when it has
 * active children and is not directly actionable.
 */
decompose(parentId: string, children: CreateWritRequest[]): Promise<WritDoc[]>
```

---

## Open Questions

- **Should `commission-post` be a permissionless tool?** It represents patron authority — commissions come from outside the guild. But Coco (running inside a session) needs to call it. Current thinking: gate it with `clerk:write` and grant that to the steward role.

- **Writ type validation — strict or advisory?** The Clerk validates against `clerk.writTypes` in config. But this means adding a new type requires a config change. Alternative: accept any string, use the config list only for documentation/tooling hints. Current thinking: strict validation — the guild should know its own vocabulary.

---

## Implementation Notes

- Standalone apparatus package at `packages/plugins/clerk/`. Requires only the Stacks.
- `WritDoc.type` uses a guild-defined vocabulary, not a framework enum. The Clerk validates against `clerk.writTypes` in the apparatus config section but the framework imposes no meaning on the type name.
- Writ ids use the format `w-{base36_timestamp}{hex_random}` — sortable by creation time, unique without coordination. Not a formal ULID, but provides the same useful properties (temporal ordering, no coordination).
- The `transition()` method is the single choke point for all status changes. All tools and future integrations go through it. This is where validation, timestamp setting, and (future) event emission and hierarchy rollup happen.
- When the Clockworks is eventually added as a recommended dependency, resolve it at emit time via `guild().apparatus()`, not at startup — so the Clerk functions with or without it.

# Writ Parent/Child Relationships

Today, writs support arbitrary relationships between each other via 'links'. We would like to add a more structured, explicit relationship as well between "parent" writs and "child" writs. Parent/Child relationships would form a DAG, with each child having zero or one parents, and parents having zero or more children. Parent and child writs can be of any type -- they do not have to be of the same type.

There is a natural relationship between the status of parents and children. The following list is illustrative and and not necessarily exhaustive--during planning, the full state machine of parent/child hierarchies should be explored and designed, and may contradict the explicit examples below if a better design is selected.

- Parent writs are in a 'pending' or 'waiting' state if they have any children in non-terminal states. (Use suitable existing state or introduce new one)
- If any child writs fail, then all siblings should be cancelled/abandoned/something, and the parent should be failed as well
- when all children for a parent are completed, the parent should transition from pending/waiting into the ready state
- etc

Wherever possible, the writ state transitions should use CDC so that parent/sibling transitions happen in the same transaction.
